rules.intent: Keep the execution brief mandatory and consistently owned by technical_manager.

all_of: `technical_manager` produces and refreshes the `Implementation Brief`, which is the execution input for `coder` and `tester`.
If `coder` or `tester` lacks a readable `Implementation Brief`, they must return `blocked` to `technical_manager`.
production and refresh of the `Implementation Brief`
if `coder` or `tester` is active, you must produce the latest `Implementation Brief` before their work starts
Before implementation, obtain the latest `Implementation Brief` path from `technical_manager`.
If the brief path is missing or the brief is unreadable, stop and return blocked to `technical_manager` instead of proceeding.
Before validation, obtain the latest `Implementation Brief` path from `technical_manager`.
If the brief path is missing or the brief is unreadable, stop and return blocked to `technical_manager` instead of proceeding.

rules.intent: Do not let coder-involved work bypass the required tester handoff.

all_of: If `coder` is active, downstream `tester` handoff is mandatory before settlement or submit.
Main-thread closeout cannot substitute for a missing required `tester` handoff once `coder` has participated.
once `coder` participates, downstream `tester` handoff is mandatory before settlement or submit
If coder participates, a downstream tester handoff is mandatory before settlement; qc is optional and cannot replace tester.
Tester is the required downstream validation owner after coder in any coder-involved chain.

rules.intent: Keep QC risk-based and downstream of the required tester handoff.

all_of: `qc` is optional by risk or explicit audit need, and cannot replace `tester`.
qc is optional and cannot replace tester.
QC remains risk-based optional and cannot replace this tester handoff.
in coder-involved chains, QC is considered only after required tester handoff, and that routing decision belongs to `technical_manager`

rules.intent: Keep progress write ownership aligned across top-level guardrails and routing.

all_of: Only `technical_manager` may write `.codex/progress/**`.
for long-running technical tasks, `technical_manager` owns `.codex/progress/**` writes and keeps `history` + `current.md` synchronized.
`technical_manager` is the only execution role that writes `.codex/progress/**`.

rules.intent: Keep hot-task lifecycle semantics aligned across routing, state shape, and long-running progress templates.

all_of: Terminal task statuses are `completed` and `cancelled` only.
Do not infer completion from silence, missing follow-up, or session end.
If a session stops while task status is `active`, `handoff`, or `blocked`, runtime hooks should record an interruption timestamp instead of changing task status.
Starting a new hot task while another non-terminal task is still open should retire the previous hot task into `recent_tasks` by default as `paused`.
if `.codex/state/task-context.json` marks `sticky_owner=technical_manager`, same-task source-code follow-up should stay on the technical-delivery line by default even when phrased as Q&A.
sticky owner means role-line continuity, not a requirement to keep the same subagent process alive across turns.
`node .codex/scripts/context/task-reconcile.mjs` is the startup helper for interrupted hot-task review. It may suggest resume or settle, but must not auto-complete tasks.
`node .codex/scripts/context/task-progress-sync.mjs`: read-only helper that reports drift between hot task state and long-running `current.md`
use `node .codex/scripts/context/task-state.mjs` for routed-task lifecycle changes in `.codex/state/task-context.json`.
when the next action depends on explicit user clarification or choice, set `status=waiting_user` instead of `blocked`.
before final closeout of a tracked task, write `status=completed` or `status=cancelled`; do not let session end imply completion.
when the user clearly starts a new task, retire the previous open hot task into `recent_tasks` by default as `paused`.
when the current hot task carries `sticky_owner=technical_manager`, keep `Aide` user-facing but preserve technical follow-up ownership on the technical-delivery line.
use `node .codex/scripts/context/task-reconcile.mjs` at startup or resume to surface interrupted-task follow-up guidance without auto-settling the task.

update `.codex/state/task-context.json` through `node .codex/scripts/context/task-state.mjs` when technical delivery starts, changes handoff owner, becomes blocked or `waiting_user`, resumes, or settles.
use `waiting_user` when the next move depends on user clarification or decision; reserve `blocked` for repository, environment, or execution constraints the system still owns.
before final technical closeout, set `completed` or `cancelled` explicitly; do not let session stop imply completion.
before switching the hot slot to another task, retire the current non-terminal task into `recent_tasks` by default as `paused`.
when the hot task is in source-code technical follow-up, keep `sticky_owner=technical_manager` until the task settles or re-triage moves ownership elsewhere.
when long-running tracking is active, use `node .codex/scripts/context/task-progress-sync.mjs` as the read-only drift check between hot task state and `.codex/progress/**`.

"checkpoint": "none"

- Status: `active|handoff|blocked|waiting_user|paused|parked|completed|cancelled`
- Status: `active|handoff|blocked|waiting_user|paused|parked|completed|cancelled`

rules.intent: Keep Implementation Brief examples and handoff paths slugged, space-free, and clearly separated from the document title.

all_of: **Suggested Path**: `plans/<task-slug>-implementation-brief.md`
`Implementation Brief` is the document type/title, not the literal filename
The file path must stay slugged and space-free, for example `plans/<task-slug>-implementation-brief.md`
**Execution Input Path**: `plans/<task-slug>-implementation-brief.md`
**Execution Input Type**: `Implementation Brief`
**Downstream Implementation Brief Path**: `[plans/<task-slug>-implementation-brief.md or N/A]`
`Implementation Brief` is the document type, not the literal filename
keep the brief path slugged and space-free, for example `plans/<task-slug>-implementation-brief.md`
downstream roles should exchange the actual path through `brief_path`, never infer a filename from the title alone
"brief_path": "plans/<task-slug>-implementation-brief.md"
Use a slugged, space-free path such as `plans/<task-slug>-implementation-brief.md`.
"brief_path": "plans/<task-slug>-implementation-brief.md"
Use a slugged, space-free path such as `plans/<task-slug>-implementation-brief.md`.
